lines: At the deepest reach the bones are not stacked. They are set. Skeletons stood upright on frames of their own ribs, hands wired into prayer, skulls crowned with carved thorn. A confession is folded into the fingers of the one in the middle.
'I promised prayer would save them. Perhaps it did, though not in any sense the Pontifex would bless. The litany pressed every plea into the bone until the dead could neither open nor answer.'

'I have set the best of them so they can go on. The diocese forgot this house and all its counts. It forgot that God lives in keeping, not in tallying. I will climb up and walk out into the road and say nothing of any of it. This stays down here: the only true thing I ever made.'
